creator: @quebert
executive_summary: Fear/Greed=43 and synchronized multi-sector RSI capitulation across tech, payments, and financials create a rare moderate-risk entry window anchored by MSFT, MA, and CRM as the highest-conviction AI and digital-payments plays with collective 54%+ upside to analyst targets. Portfolio holds 11 positions across 5 sectors with correlation caps enforced, NU reduced to 5% pending NPL clarity, and HD designated watch-only ahead of the May 19 earnings binary.

macro_environment.summary: MIXED regime with VIX=18.52 and Fear/Greed=43 (Fear zone) signals broad sentiment overshoot; SPY down -0.51% on May 15 creates opportunistic entries across deeply oversold tech, financials, and payments names while the AI capex narrative accelerates to a $200B revenue target.
key_factors: AI hyperscaler capex race intensifying — revenue forecasts reach $200B by 2027, directly benefiting META, MSFT, and CRM Agentforce
Fear/Greed=43 historically precedes 3-6 month mean-reversion rallies in MIXED regimes
Warsh Fed nomination signals bank deregulation narrative — structural tailwind for WFC asset cap removal and BAC balance sheet leverage
Treasury yields at 4.42% compressing multiples but plateauing; rate stability reduces duration risk in quality growth names
LatAm digital finance penetration accelerating — 100M+ unbanked consumers entering formal banking creates decade-long secular runway

cross_sector_insights.insight: Multi-sector RSI capitulation: META (25.1), PYPL (22.6), NU (21.2), WFC (30.1), BAC (32.0) — five names across three sectors simultaneously printing extreme oversold readings.
cross_sector_insights.implication: Synchronized capitulation across uncorrelated sectors typically precedes a 10-15% mean-reversion rally; this amplifies entry quality for moderate-risk portfolios building on a down SPY day.

cross_sector_insights.insight: AI infrastructure spend is creating concurrent winners at multiple stack layers — hyperscalers, cloud platforms, and enterprise SaaS.
cross_sector_insights.implication: META (ad targeting moat), MSFT (Azure AI), and CRM (Agentforce enterprise agents) each monetize the same $200B AI wave through distinct revenue channels, reducing effective concentration risk despite thematic overlap.

cross_sector_insights.insight: Warsh Fed nomination is a structural deregulation signal independent of the rate path.
cross_sector_insights.implication: WFC (asset cap removal) and BAC (balance sheet leverage unlock) trade at sub-10x fwdPE with an embedded call option on regulatory easing; correlation cap limits to 2 picks, but both offer asymmetric risk-reward.

cross_sector_insights.insight: LatAm fintech (NU + MELI) provides geographic diversification uncorrelated with US rate cycles.
cross_sector_insights.implication: 27 consecutive quarters of >30% growth (MELI) and 100M+ customers (NU) represent structural penetration, but combined BRL exposure caps the pair at ~13% with active FX monitoring required.

cross_sector_insights.insight: REGN gene therapy approval (Otarmeni) differentiates healthcare holdings from pure defensive plays.
cross_sector_insights.implication: AMGN provides dividend ballast while REGN adds pipeline growth optionality; together they satisfy the healthcare correlation cap with complementary beta profiles across the MIXED regime.

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Microsoft
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: OpenAI $38B revenue cap restructure removes the primary overhang; +3.84% on May 14 with +4.35% alpha confirms institutional re-rating is underway.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Azure AI growth (>30% YoY target) combined with the OpenAI restructure unlocking unbounded revenue sharing creates a multi-year compounding engine; Copilot enterprise adoption serves as the near-term catalyst sustaining the trade above the $390 structural support.
thesis_invalidators: Adverse Musk v. OpenAI verdict reversing restructure terms
UK CMA Teams/Office unbundling expanding to US jurisdiction
Azure YoY growth drops below 30% in Q4 FY2026 earnings

financial_health.health_note: Best-in-class balance sheet with $75B+ net cash; FCF margin ~35%; minimal default risk across all macro scenarios

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Mastercard
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Bullish sentiment with strong buy consensus and $647 target; +2.1% alpha on a down SPY day validates institutional conviction in the digital payments secular thesis.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: MA's network-effect moat and cross-border fee engine benefit from EM digital payment adoption with 29.7% upside to analyst target; asset-light model ensures margin expansion as volume scales without proportional capital deployment.
thesis_invalidators: US PIX-style instant payment system forcing Brazil/LatAm market share loss
Global merchant fee compression via coordinated regulatory pressure
Recession causing cross-border travel and discretionary spending contraction >20%

financial_health.health_note: Capital-light franchise with strong FCF; manageable debt relative to earnings power; no credit loss exposure

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Salesforce
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Agentforce enterprise AI platform delivered +4.0% / +4.5% alpha on May 14, confirming institutional conviction; earnings in 12 days create a defined near-term binary catalyst with 54% upside to analyst target.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Agentforce positions CRM as the enterprise AI execution layer for Fortune 500 automation at scale; Q1 earnings in ~12 days (est. May 27) are the high-conviction catalyst — if Agentforce ARR metrics beat, analyst target of $267 becomes the near-term anchor.
thesis_invalidators: Agentforce customer churn >20% within first two contract cycles signals product-market fit failure
Microsoft/ServiceNow displacing CRM in enterprise AI agent RFPs at scale
Q2 revenue guidance cut implying <8% YoY growth — thesis requires double-digit growth

financial_health.health_note: Subscription revenue provides high visibility; FCF margins improving as Agentforce scales without proportional COGS increase

risk_adjusted_picks.name: MercadoLibre
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: 27 consecutive quarters above 30% growth confirmed by news; Goldman Sachs maintains buy with $2,236 target; +1.35% alpha on May 14 in a down market demonstrates institutional support.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: MELI is the Amazon + PayPal of LatAm operating in structurally underpenetrated markets with 50M+ Mercado Pago users forming a fintech flywheel that compounds as formal banking adoption accelerates across Brazil, Mexico, and Argentina.
thesis_invalidators: BRL devaluation >20% materially compressing USD-reported earnings for 2 consecutive quarters
Q2 revenue miss driven by confirmed competitive market share loss in Brazilian e-commerce
Banco Central do Brasil imposing Mercado Pago fintech operational restrictions

financial_health.health_note: Elevated leverage from fintech subsidiary funding; offset by 30%+ revenue CAGR and strong operating leverage trajectory — growth profile compensates for Z-score gray zone

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Meta Platforms
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=25.1 extreme oversold with strong buy consensus and $826 analyst target (34% upside); earnings overhang already cleared (earnings_days_away=-16).
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Meta's AI hyperscaler capex is directly monetizable through ad targeting precision and Llama licensing revenue; at fwdPE=16.94 with RSI=25.1, the market has overcorrected on regulatory fears — consensus targets imply 34% upside with earnings overhang already cleared.
thesis_invalidators: FTC antitrust ruling forcing Instagram or WhatsApp divestiture
AI compute costs exceeding ad revenue growth for 2 consecutive reported quarters
EU or US AI-ad restriction legislation materially limiting targeting capabilities

financial_health.health_note: Net cash positive with $50B+ active buyback; FCF yield ~4%; zero reliance on capital markets — regulatory risk is operational, not financial

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Wells Fargo
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Asset cap removal in 2026 is the single most underappreciated catalyst in US banking; at fwdPE=9.39 and RSI=30, WFC trades at a structural discount with $96 analyst target (30% upside).
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Federal Reserve asset cap removal unlocks $200B+ in balance sheet deployment capacity; the Warsh Fed deregulatory stance adds a second catalyst layer — NIM expansion plus operational freedom creates a dual-catalyst path to the analyst target.
thesis_invalidators: Fed formally extends asset cap beyond Q4 2026 with no removal timeline
CRE loan portfolio deterioration accelerating in Q2 beyond consensus reserve estimates
Consumer mortgage credit quality deterioration exceeding 2020 peak loss rates

financial_health.health_note: CET1 ratio well above regulatory minimums; asset cap is a regulatory constraint on growth, not a credit quality signal

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Bank of America
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Strong buy consensus with $62.98 target (26% upside); Warsh Fed deregulation trade confirmed by news; RSI=32 at fwdPE=9.88.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: BAC is the purest large-cap play on Warsh Fed bank deregulation with its scale consumer and institutional franchise; Buffett-backed anchor ownership provides a fundamental floor while the stock trades at a significant discount to intrinsic value.
thesis_invalidators: Warsh nomination fails Senate confirmation, reverting to restrictive Fed posture
Consumer credit card delinquency rate exceeds 4% triggering material loss reserve builds
Legacy mortgage securities litigation creating unexpected liability

financial_health.health_note: G-SIB with CET1 >11.5%; Berkshire Hathaway anchor stake provides fundamental backing

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Otarmeni gene therapy approval confirmed in news — pipeline maturation catalyst at RSI=37.3 and fwdPE=13.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: Dupixent franchise generates $12B+ annual royalties with patent runway to 2031 while Otarmeni approval signals pipeline maturation beyond a single franchise; at 24.9% upside to analyst target, REGN offers biotech growth compounding at value-sector multiples.
thesis_invalidators: Dupixent biosimilar receives FDA approval before 2028
FDA issues safety hold on Otarmeni post-approval based on gene therapy signal
IRA drug price negotiation expansion explicitly targeting Dupixent

financial_health.health_note: Cash-rich with $15B+ net cash; R&D pipeline funded entirely by Dupixent cash flows

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Amgen
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Defensive dividend compounder at RSI=41.4 near support; AMG-133 obesity Phase 3 provides a binary upside option on the largest drug market of the decade.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: AMGN functions as the portfolio shock absorber — 3.2% dividend yield with AMG-133 obesity program creating a free call option; downside is structurally cushioned by cash flows while upside depends on Phase 3 readout timing in 2026.
thesis_invalidators: AMG-133 Phase 3 primary endpoint failure eliminating the obesity pipeline option
IRA drug price negotiation expanding to Repatha or Enbrel
FDA clinical hold on AMG-133 based on cardiovascular safety signal

financial_health.health_note: Elevated leverage from historical buyback program (~$65B debt) offsets Z-score; $10B+ annual FCF provides full debt service coverage

risk_adjusted_picks.name: PayPal Holdings
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: RSI=22.6 capitulation territory; +0.31% alpha on a down SPY day suggests institutional accumulation beginning; DOJ settlement and crypto pivot are genuine reset catalysts.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: PYPL at RSI=22.6 and fwdPE=7.78 is priced for structural collapse; DOJ settlement resolution and crypto-shift business model reset create a mean-reversion trade with 16.8% upside — sized conservatively given insider distribution and hold consensus.
thesis_invalidators: Q2 revenue guidance cut signaling structural decline, not cyclical trough
Visa/Mastercard checkout displacing PYPL in >30% of web transactions within 12 months
CEO departure creating management continuity uncertainty

financial_health.health_note: Net cash positive; strong FCF generation; underperformance is purely sentiment/competitive-fear driven, not credit or liquidity risk

risk_adjusted_picks.name: Nu Holdings
risk_adjusted_picks.reasoning: Q1 revenue beat ($5B+) but margin compression triggered -5.61% selloff; RSI=21.2 extreme oversold with PEG=0.81 and $19.87 analyst target (62% upside) — reduced pending NPL clarity.
risk_adjusted_picks.thesis: NU's 100M+ customer base at sub-$12 represents a structural entry for the fastest-growing digital bank in LatAm; revenue beat confirms top-line strength intact, while margin pressure is treated as a management execution risk until Q1 full filing confirms NPL trajectory.
thesis_invalidators: Q1 full 20-F filing reveals NPL ratio >7%, signaling credit cycle deterioration
Brazilian Central Bank imposing consumer credit rate caps materially compressing NIM
BRL devaluation >15% compressing USD-reported metrics for 2 consecutive quarters

financial_health.health_note: Rapid-growth fintech with improving but volatile credit metrics; Q1 margin compression warrants monitoring

historical_accuracy.notable: MSFT +3.84% (+4.35% alpha) on OpenAI restructure and CRM +4.0% (+4.5% alpha) on Agentforce momentum were standout winners on a down SPY day; NU -5.61% (-5.1% alpha) was the primary detractor on Q1 margin miss post-earnings; 5 of 11 picks beat SPY on alpha basis including PYPL (+0.31% alpha) despite negative absolute return

warnings: HD: Earnings May 19 — do NOT enter pre-print; wait for post-earnings reaction before establishing any position.
NU: Q1 margin compression confirmed — position capped at 5% until full Q1 20-F filing clarifies NPL ratio; hard exit if NPL >7%.
CRM: Earnings ~12 days out (est. May 27) — trim from 10% to 7% if pre-earnings price run exceeds 8% from entry.
Financials correlation cap enforced: C excluded despite buy rating — hard limit of 2 financials picks (WFC + BAC only).
LatAm FX concentration: NU (5%) + MELI (8%) = 13% combined BRL exposure — trim MELI to 5% if BRL weakens >10% vs USD on sustained basis.
Big tech cap applied: META is the sole screened big_tech new entry; MSFT (cloud/AI) continued — monitor combined tech concentration above 27% of equity allocation.
